question_text: A student burns 500 J of food energy during a 10-second sprint. Where does most of this energy go?

options.label: A
options.text: Into making the muscles contract and move the body, with some converted to heat

options.label: B
options.text: Only into movement with no energy wasted

options.label: C
options.text: Into the student's bones and joints

options.label: D
options.text: Into the air as sound energy

correct_answer: Into making the muscles contract and move the body, with some converted to heat
explanation: Muscles are only about 25% efficient, meaning most food energy becomes heat (which is why you get hot when exercising), while the rest does mechanical work to move the body. This demonstrates energy conservation—energy isn't lost, just transferred and transformed.
